sentences.text: Dybt under Kronborg Slot ligger en kæmpe kriger, som hedder Holger Danske.
sentences.translation: Deep under Kronborg Castle lies a giant warrior, who is called Holger Danske.

words.grammar: en kæmpe (a giant, noun) · kæmpe- (giant-, used as a prefix/adj.) · kæmpestor = gigantic
en kriger · krigeren (the warrior) · krigere (plural)

sentences.text: Han har siddet der i mange hundrede år og sover tungt.
sentences.translation: He has been sitting there for many hundred years and sleeps heavily.

words.grammar: sidde (to sit) · sad (past) · har siddet (perfect) · present perfect = action started in the past, continuing now

sentences.text: Hans skæg er vokset langt ned og har viklet sig om bordet foran ham.
sentences.translation: His beard has grown long down and has wound itself around the table in front of him.

words.grammar: vokse (to grow) · voksede (past) · er vokset (perfect, takes "er" not "har") · motion/change-of-state verb
et skæg · skægget (the beard) · skæg (plural, unchanged)

sentences.text: Ifølge legenden vil Holger Danske vågne, hvis Danmark nogensinde er i stor fare.
sentences.translation: According to the legend, Holger Danske will wake up if Denmark is ever in great danger.

words.grammar: ifølge + noun = according to · ifølge legenden = according to the legend
hvis (conditional conjunction) · introduces a condition · followed by present tense for future possibility

sentences.text: Da han var ung, kæmpede han modigt for kongen og for landet.
sentences.translation: When he was young, he fought bravely for the king and for the country.

words.grammar: kæmpe (to fight) · kæmpede (past) · har kæmpet (perfect) · weak verb
modig (brave, adj.) · modigt (adverb/neuter form)

sentences.text: Selvom han nu sover, holder han stadig sit sværd fast i hånden.
sentences.translation: Even though he now sleeps, he still holds his sword firmly in his hand.

words.grammar: selvom (subordinating, concessive) · admits one fact while contrasting with another
et sværd · sværdet (the sword) · sværd (plural, unchanged)

sentences.text: Mange børn har hørt historien om ham, når de besøgte slottet om sommeren.
sentences.translation: Many children have heard the story about him, when they visited the castle in the summer.

words.grammar: når (subordinating) · used for repeated or habitual events, in any tense — contrast with da for a single past event

sentences.text: Nogle siger, at man kan høre ham snorke dybt nede i kælderen.
sentences.translation: Some say that you can hear him snore deep down in the cellar.

words.grammar: man (impersonal/generic subject pronoun) · used where English often says "you" or "people"
en kælder · kælderen (the cellar) · kældre (plural)

sentences.text: Under Anden Verdenskrig brugte den danske modstandsbevægelse hans navn som symbol på frihed.
sentences.translation: During the Second World War, the Danish resistance movement used his name as a symbol of freedom.

words.grammar: en modstandsbevægelse · modstandsbevægelsen (the resistance movement) · compound: modstand (resistance) + bevægelse (movement)
en frihed · friheden (the freedom)

sentences.text: Statuen af Holger Danske, som man kan se i kælderen i dag, blev lavet af billedhuggeren H.P. Pedersen-Dan.
sentences.translation: The statue of Holger Danske, which one can see in the cellar today, was made by the sculptor H.P. Pedersen-Dan.

words.grammar: som (relative pronoun, here as object of "se") · can often be dropped in casual speech when used as object, but kept here
blive lavet af (passive: to be made by) · blive + past participle + af (agent) = standard Danish passive

sentences.text: Hvis du går ned ad de mørke trapper, kan du selv møde den sovende kæmpe.
sentences.translation: If you go down the dark stairs, you can meet the sleeping giant yourself.

words.grammar: sove (to sleep) → sovende (present participle/adjective) · -ende ending, like English -ing
en trappe · trappen (the stairs) · trapper (plural)

sentences.text: Indtil da venter Holger Danske tålmodigt, klar til at forsvare Danmark igen.
sentences.translation: Until then, Holger Danske waits patiently, ready to defend Danmark again.

words.grammar: tålmodig (patient, adj.) · tålmodigt (adverb/neuter form)
forsvare (to defend) · forsvarer (present) · forsvarede (past) · har forsvaret (perfect)

metadata.adaptedFrom: Danish national legend (Ogier the Dane), adapted for upper-A2/B1 learners

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Holger Danske vil vågne, hvis Danmark nogensinde er i stor fare.
grammarPoints.explanation: Hvis introduces a condition. Danish typically uses present tense even when describing a future possibility — there's no separate future conditional form needed.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Selvom han nu sover, holder han stadig sit sværd fast.
grammarPoints.explanation: Selvom admits one fact while contrasting it with something unexpected. The subordinate clause can come first, with the main clause verb following directly after the comma.

grammarPoints.rule: Present perfect for ongoing past-to-present states
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Han har siddet der i mange hundrede år.
grammarPoints.explanation: Har/er + past participle describes something that started in the past and is still true now — distinct from simple past (sad), which would imply the sitting is finished.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Statuen blev lavet af billedhuggeren H.P. Pedersen-Dan.
grammarPoints.explanation: This is the standard way to form the passive in spoken/everyday Danish: blive + past participle, with af introducing who performed the action.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Da han var ung... / ...når de besøgte slottet om sommeren.
grammarPoints.explanation: Da is for a single, completed event in the past. Når is for repeated or habitual events (in any tense) and for present/future "when".

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Ifølge legenden vil Holger Danske vågne...
grammarPoints.explanation: Ifølge is a simple preposition for citing a source — legenden, eksperterne, avisen — followed directly by the noun, no extra connecting word needed.
